methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Quaise Energy, a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Carlos Araque and Matt Houde, has raised $75M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector comparables (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M), supplemented by a broader universe of 8 private-sector peers and 1 public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $75M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for Series A advancement, yields $150M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 direct comparables produces a median valuation of $150M, with a range of $110M to $200M. Both methods converge precisely on $150M, supporting triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M (low) to $172M (high), with a median point estimate of $150M. Key uncertainties include the granularity of comparable transaction terms, the extent to which public-market signals (represented by LNZA) apply to private frontier-tech valuations in this sector, and the degree to which Quaise's specific technology roadmap and capital efficiency justify positioning within the peer range rather than at its boundaries.
